heading: Get Enough Sleep for Healthy Hair
text: Getting enough sleep plays an important role in maintaining overall health, including the health of your scalp and hair. Aim for 7–8 hours of quality sleep each night, follow a consistent sleep schedule, and limit screen time before bedtime. Small healthy habits can contribute to healthier-looking hair over time.

heading: Use a wide-tooth comb
text: Using a wide-tooth comb helps gently detangle your hair and reduces unnecessary breakage. Start combing from the ends, work your way up slowly, and be gentle with wet or tangled hair.

heading: Avoid combing wet hair
text: Wet hair breaks more easily. Use a wide-tooth comb, detangle gently, and let hair dry slightly before combing.

heading: Healthy hair begins with healthy nutrition
text: What you eat plays an important role in maintaining healthy hair. Include foods rich in protein, nuts & seeds, leafy greens, and fruits. A balanced diet, along with a consistent hair care routine, supports healthy-looking hair.

heading: Hydration matters for healthy hair
text: Healthy hair starts from within. Drinking 2-3 litres of water every day helps support your overall health, including your scalp and hair. Pair good hydration with a consistent hair care routine for the best results.

heading: Oil massage is the key to healthy hair
text: Regular oil massage helps nourish the scalp and keeps hair soft and manageable. Apply Nila Herbal Hair Oil, gently massage for 5-10 minutes, and leave it for at least 1 hour or overnight.
